subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Join the Skill World design crew to use reason about shape attributes, quadrilaterals, equal partitions, and fractions from shapes in real classroom missions.
lesson.mini_lesson: Reason about shape attributes, quadrilaterals, equal partitions, and fractions from shapes.
lesson.objective: Reason about shape attributes, quadrilaterals, equal partitions, and fractions from shapes.
lesson.concept_explanation: Use models and precise math language to reason about shape attributes, quadrilaterals, equal partitions, and fractions from shapes. Explain the strategy and include units when the answer needs them.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may think only rectangles count as quadrilaterals.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help with schedules, materials, maps, art projects, and data from everyday activities.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the task, choose the model, solve carefully, and check that the answer matches the question.
hints: Read what the question asks.
Use the visual model to organize the information.
Check that your answer includes the right unit or name.

misconception_bank.quadrilateral_confusion: Learners may think only rectangles count as quadrilaterals.
misconception_bank.attribute_overgeneralization: Learners may sort by how a shape looks instead of its attributes.
misconception_bank.unequal_partition_error: Learners may call unequal pieces equal parts.
misconception_bank.fraction_shape_confusion: Learners may count shaded pieces without checking total equal parts.

game_theme.title: Skill World: Shapes, Attributes, and Partitioning
game_theme.mission_intro: Build confidence with shapes, attributes, and partitioning through a guided mission and focused practice levels.
